name: Asparagus in Its Own Juices
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the tough bottom end of the asparagus and discard it.
2. Peel the stalks with a vegetable peeler.
3. Place the asparagus in a skillet or saucepan that is large enough to hold them lying down and barely cover with cold water.
4. Add butter and salt, then bring to a boil over high heat.
5. Stir the asparagus occasionally so it cooks evenly.
6. The asparagus is done when the water has evaporated, leaving only the concentrated, buttery juice of the asparagus.
7. Grind fresh pepper to taste over the asparagus and arrange on a vegetable platter.
